title: Module 3 quiz: ControlNet and guided generation

questions.prompt: ControlNet type for replicating exact human pose?

choices.text: OpenPose. Detects skeleton + joints from reference; ControlNet uses skeleton to constrain generated pose. Reference photo → OpenPose preprocessor → skeleton image → ControlNet + prompt = new character in exact pose. Essential for character consistency, action poses, reference matching. Stack with Canny for outline + pose; Depth for 3D layout + pose.

questions.explanation: OpenPose. Skeleton detection; pose preservation across generations. Multi-ControlNet stacking for combined control.

questions.prompt: Fix bad hands on generated image without regenerating whole thing?

choices.text: Inpaint with mask on hands + hand-focused prompt + denoising 0.6-0.8 + mask blur. Optional: inpaint-specific model, hand LoRA, ControlNet hand depth or OpenPose hand. Pro workflow iterates: base generation → inpaint problem areas → final composite. Standard polish technique.

questions.explanation: Inpaint masked hands area + prompt + 0.6-0.8 denoising + mask blur. Standard polish pattern.

questions.prompt: Denoising strength for img2img style transfer?

choices.text: 0.5-0.7 typical. Preserves composition + applies new style. Lower (0.1-0.3) too subtle; higher (0.9+) loses original. For style transfer (photo → painting, sketch → render): 0.5-0.7. For minor changes: 0.1-0.3. For major reinterpretation: 0.7-0.85. Tune per goal.

questions.explanation: 0.5-0.7 for style transfer. Preserves composition + applies new style. Lower = subtle; higher = lose original.

questions.prompt: Same character (same face) across 20 illustrations. Approach?

choices.text: Face IP-Adapter (or train character LoRA for ongoing series). IP-Adapter Face encodes reference face features into each generation; strength tunable. Combined with character LoRA for stronger lock. For one-off: IP-Adapter sufficient; for ongoing series: LoRA + IP-Adapter combined.

questions.explanation: Face IP-Adapter for one-off; train character LoRA for ongoing series. Pro pattern: combine both for strongest consistency.

choices.text: Use an image as part of the prompt — encode reference image's features (style, content, identity, mood) as conditioning alongside text. Variants: standard (style/content), Face (identity), FaceID. Strength tunable; weight types (linear, style-only). For style transfer + character consistency + composition reference + mood matching.

questions.explanation: Image-as-prompt. Reference image's features encoded as conditioning. Style + Face variants. Production-grade reference workflows.
